commit.message:
Add full finance toolkit — 7 tools in one app

- app.py: home page with tool overview
- pages/1: 5-Year Financial Model (moved from root)
- pages/2: Annual Budget (existing)
- pages/3: Lending Calculator (loan + mortgage with early repayment)
- pages/4: Portfolio Analysis (Sharpe, VaR, risk/return scatter)
- pages/5: Stock Comparison (2 stocks, correlation, ratios)
- pages/6: Commodities Dashboard (gold, silver, oil, gas, copper, wheat)
- pages/7: Stock Analysis (any ticker, MAs, volatility, company info)

All charts rebuilt in Plotly. Consistent styling across all pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Annual Budget tool (1-year P&L with monthly breakdown)

- Sales forecast, direct costs, overheads by category
- Monthly P&L table with FY totals and % of revenue
- KPI cards: Revenue, Gross Profit, Overheads, EBITDA
- 4 charts: Revenue vs Costs, Monthly EBITDA, Overhead breakdown, Cumulative EBITDA
- Excel template download + upload
- PDF export with branded report

Anchor tower to bottom so words build up from foundation

justify-end pb-8: single word sits at screen bottom, subsequent
words stack upward — visually builds from the ground up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Dynamic rise distance: each row rises from proportional depth below base

Bottom row rises 40px, top row rises (N-1)*50+40px — words physically
travel from the foundation level up to their final position.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Increase towerRowIn translateY for more dramatic rise from base

60px travel distance makes each row visibly rise up from below
instead of barely-noticeable 20px nudge.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix word placement to build tower from base

Iterate placement loop bottom→top so base rows (wide, appear first in
animation) win ties over observation deck rows. Big/prominent words now
land at the foundation and visibly appear first as the tower builds up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Make tower build animation visibly bottom-to-top

Increase row stagger from 40ms to 100ms and reduce per-row duration
from 500ms to 400ms, making the cascading bottom-to-top build effect
clearly perceivable (~1.1s total for 8 rows).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Extend profanity filter with anatomical and explicit terms

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add host_wake_all_slots to pre-warm shadow FX before Song Mode playback

With Link Audio, Move's native track audio routes through shadow slots
for FX processing. When a track goes quiet, the slot idles and skips
rendering. On step transitions, the sudden audio arrival caused glitches
because FX state was stale. Wake all slots at playback start and each
step advance so FX chains run on zeros before audio arrives.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Port beamtalk_interface and beamtalk_workspace_interface tests to Phase 2 primitives (BT-1109) (#1156)

## Summary

Ports test coverage from Phase 1 gen_server interfaces to Phase 2
primitives modules, preparing for safe deletion of the gen_server
wrappers per BT-1093.

- **`beamtalk_interface_primitives_tests.erl`** (24 tests) — Tests
`dispatch/3` for all selectors: `allClasses`, `classNamed:`, `globals`,
`help:`, `help:selector:`, `version`, plus unknown selector error
handling
- **`beamtalk_workspace_interface_primitives_tests.erl`** (26 tests) —
Tests `dispatch/3` for all selectors: `actors`, `actorAt:`, `classes`,
`load:`, `globals`, `bind:as:`, `unbind:`, plus `get_user_bindings/0`
and `get_session_bindings/0` external API
- Marks existing Phase 1 test files as `@deprecated` referencing BT-1093

## Linear Issue

https://linear.app/beamtalk/issue/BT-1109

## Test Plan

- [x] `just test` passes (2668 Erlang tests, 0 failures)
- [x] `just build && just clippy && just fmt-check` passes
- [x] All 24 interface primitives tests pass
- [x] All 26 workspace interface primitives tests pass

commit.message:
fix(defi): correct DefiLlama protocol slugs (Sky/GMX/Pendle/Aave)

- TRACKED_PROTOCOLS: remove broken slugs (maker→sky-lending, gmx-v2→gmx-v2-perps,
  drop aave-v3-arbitrum+pendle-arbitrum which don't exist in either API)
- Add sparklend (Sky ecosystem, $1.3B TVL)
- Add _PROTOCOL_API_SLUG_MAP to bridge pool slugs → protocol API slugs
- check_protocol_health(): use _PROTOCOL_API_SLUG_MAP.get(slug, slug)
- _LENDING_PROTOCOL_SLUGS + _PROTO_KEY_MAP: replace maker→sky-lending+sparklend
- docs: PQ_JWT_SECRET must be identical on Both machines, reference sync script

Fixes: 400 errors + 60s+ timeouts causing DeFi tab to load empty.
MakerDAO rebranded to Sky (slug change confirmed via yields API).
Chain-specific slugs (aave-v3-arbitrum) don't exist in protocol API.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Skip crop for null-box events, guard against degenerate crop bounds

Events with box=null (audio events, in-progress events) are stored with
w=h=0. Attempting to crop a 0x0 region caused SystemError from PIL.
Now skips crop when w or h is zero, and double-checks right>left and
bottom>top after clamping to image bounds before cropping.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Refactor shift regeneration to atomic backend transaction (Fase 1)

Move shift regeneration from frontend multi-request approach to single
POST /users/:id/apply-schedule endpoint that handles everything in one
database transaction: delete old auto-shifts, clear auto-blocks, and
generate new shifts based on weekSchedules.

commit.message:
fix: handle macOS Apple clang OpenMP detection in build.rs

Apple clang doesn't support -fopenmp directly. On macOS, detect
libomp via brew and pass the appropriate CMake variables so
find_package(OpenMP) works with -Xpreprocessor -fopenmp.

destructive commands (rm -rf, force push main) instantly. Ambiguous
calls go to Qwen3 14B (think:false, ~800ms). Fail-open on timeout.
74 new tests, 516 total passing.

commit.message:
Phase 1 Step 7: Goodreads Import — two-step flow, batch processing, progress UI

- /import page: instructions → file drop → instant CSV preview (papaparse)
  → live progress bar → redirect to /library on completion
- POST /api/import: creates imports record, returns import_id
- POST /api/import/[id]: processes rows in batches of 25 (5 concurrent),
  matching by isbn_13 → Google Books ISBN → Google Books title+author →
  minimal book record; upserts user_books and records import_rows
- PATCH /api/import/[id]: finalises import with matched/unmatched counts
- app-nav: added "Import from Goodreads" to user dropdown

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Expand news sources, add LLM scoring layer, fix cover art to 3000x3000

- Add 6 new source fetchers: Hacker News, TechCrunch/Verge/Ars/VentureBeat/
  Wired/MIT Tech Review RSS, Reuters/AP wire, CNBC RSS, Reddit Atom, ESPN RSS
- Add _safe_request() headers param for Reddit User-Agent support
- Add score_and_rank_articles() GPT-4o scoring layer (1-100 per article)
  between deduplication and selection; articles now sorted by relevance score
- Raw article yield: ~100 → ~225 per run
- main() step count 7 → 8
- Regenerate cover.png at 3000x3000 (Apple Podcasts minimum is 1400x1400)

commit.message:
fix: 使用 _worker.js 替代 _redirects 处理 Demo SPA 路由

_redirects 的 200 rewrite 被 Cloudflare Pretty URLs 干扰,
导致 308 重定向改变浏览器 URL, React Router 找不到路由。
改用 Pages Advanced Mode _worker.js, 通过 env.ASSETS.fetch()
直接返回 demo/index.html 内容, 完全绕过 Pretty URLs 问题。

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(workflows): cancel-in-progress true→false — pushes were killing scheduled runs - evening_slate_lock.yml: cancel-in-progress false (was true) - daily_briefing.yml: cancel-in-progress false (was true) - injury_refresh.yml: cancel-in-progress false (was true) Root cause: on:push + cancel-in-progress:true = push phantom run cancels real scheduled run, then skips all jobs. Net result = nothing runs. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: complexity_router crashes on list-format message content (OpenAI multi-part messages) (#22761)

* fix: complexity_router fails on list-format message content (OpenAI multi-part messages)

When a client sends messages with list-format content
(e.g. [{"type": "text", "text": "..."}] as used by the OpenAI JS SDK
and other clients), the complexity_router's async_pre_routing_hook
skipped those messages because it only handled str content. This caused
user_message to be None, the hook returned None, and the router fell
through to selecting the complexity_router deployment itself
(model="auto_router/complexity_router") which litellm cannot dispatch,
resulting in LiteLLMUnknownProvider.

Fixes:
- Extract text from list-format content parts (type=text) before
  classifying
- Return default_model instead of None when no user message can be
  extracted, preventing the crash fallthrough
- Loosen PreRoutingHookResponse.messages type from Dict[str, str] to
  Dict[str, Any] to accommodate list-format content values

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update messages type annotation in async_pre_routing_hook to Dict[str, Any]

Consistent with PreRoutingHookResponse.messages type change and the
list-format content support added in the previous commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: normalize None content to empty string in complexity_router message parsing

msg.get("content", "") returns None when the key exists with value None
(e.g. assistant messages with tool calls). Use `or ""` to normalize
None to an empty string explicitly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: strip whitespace from joined list content parts in complexity_router

Prevents leading/trailing spaces when some content parts have empty
text values (e.g. " ".join(["", "hello"]) → " hello").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Fix pre-auth key expire/delete and API key delete error handling

commit.message:
[PHASE-4.3] feat: Attachment management, compliance intelligence, and contract lifecycle

Attachment management:
- AttachmentOverride + AttachmentEditHistory models — rename solicitation attachments with full audit trail
- AttachmentFormData model — stores AI analysis results and PDF AcroForm field values
- GET /attachments now merges formData (AI name, confidence, isForm, formType, fields) into RichAttachment
- POST /attachments/analyze — GPT-4o batch analysis for AI-suggested names + form detection (SF-1449, DD-254, etc.)
- PATCH /attachments/[id] — rename with extension lock, duplicate check, SAM.gov fallback via getOpportunityAttachments
- PATCH /attachments/[id]/form-data — save AcroForm field values
- FormFillModal — PDF.js canvas renderer with AcroForm overlay inputs, multi-page navigation
- OpportunitySummaryPanel: auto-analyze on load, AI name suggestion pills, FORM badge, Fill Form button

Sidebar & opportunity page overhaul:
- Replaced DocumentDirectory sidebar with OpportunitySidebar tile grid
- Dark "Days Left" tile (centered, text-3xl) + agency wrap + data source attribution on bid estimates
- POC extracted from rawData and displayed in sidebar
- USASpending source/confidence shown under bid estimate tiles

Overview panel:
- extendedOverview field added to OpportunityBrief — 4-7 paragraph AI narrative on scope, context, risks, success metrics
- "From Solicitation" raw description is now a collapsible expansion (hidden by default)
- Contract Lifecycle & Action Plan — 4-5 collapsible phases (Pre-Award → Award/Kickoff → Performance → Option Years → Closeout)
  - Each phase: concrete action steps tailored to brief data (clearances, on-site, deliverables, option years)
  - "Ways to overdeliver" toggle per phase (amber tips for standing out with CO/COR)

Compliance tab (ScopeOverviewPanel → Compliance & Post-Award):
- Tab renamed from "Scope" to "Compliance"
- 52-term federal contracting glossary in lib/data/compliance-glossary.ts (8 categories, GlossaryTerm type)
- Contextual "?" term explainer buttons on every compliance/deliverable card — only appear when the term
  is detected in the actual item text (FAT, ITP, CDRL, WAWF, CUI, DD-254, FAR 52.XXX, plain phrases)
- Inline TermExplainer: full plain-English explanation + contractor action list + common mistakes + timing + FAR ref
- Fixed matching: handles plurals (CDRLs), spelled-out forms ("Inspection & Test Plans"), FAR clause numbers
- Any FAR/DFARS clause number auto-links to relevant glossary entry
- "📚 Reference" tab — full searchable glossary browser (52 terms, 8 categories)
- Persistent "Reference" link in compliance header — accessible from every tab

commit.message:
Replace SSE with streamable HTTP MCP endpoint on Flask

Drop the broken SSE transport and use a simple POST /mcp endpoint
that handles MCP JSON-RPC directly. Claude.ai tries POST first
(streamable HTTP) before falling back to SSE, so this works without
needing long-lived connections. Back to gunicorn + Flask.

PROMPT #248

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: demo APK coexist conflict — unique sharedUserId and provider authorities

Root cause: TERMUX_PACKAGE_NAME was always "com.termux" for all build types,
including demo. Android blocks installation when two apps share the same:
  - android:sharedUserId (UID conflict)
  - content provider authority (INSTALL_FAILED_CONFLICTING_PROVIDER)
    - com.termux.documents
    - com.termux.files

Fix: set manifestPlaceholders.TERMUX_PACKAGE_NAME = "com.termux.demo" in the
demo build type. This makes the demo's sharedUserId and provider authorities
unique (com.termux.demo.*), allowing it to install alongside NewTermux or
original Termux without any conflict.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
